Hey guys, JB here. These weekend motivations are really about just giving you cool things to think about food for thought. Have a great day and let's do some serious butt kicking on Monday. All right? Take care. You're out there and you're closer on a product, right? And they're done. You sell them. It's a $2,000 product, right? And you shake hands and say, great. You're gonna love this, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, right? So the question is, do you want to ask them right at the table, right at the closing table either it's in person or over the phone? Or do you want to wait a few days and then you call them back to check on them and say, hey, do you want to make sure everything's going well? Do you have any questions? Do you want to make sure you're having a good experience? Oh, by the way, you know, anybody else who could use the same blah, blah, blah, right? You want to do it then? Or do you need to even wait longer than that? And they just thought for the sake of, you know, politeness in general, like, you know, just being a good person that you want to wait to give the person a chance to experience the benefits of the product for themselves before they have to recommend to a friend. Which do you think is right? Now hopefully you know a little bit about this program by now. And if you said the last one, you know, I'm going to jump through the lens and try to choke you, right? Because it's terrible. You'll destroy yourself. You don't have to wait for any arbitrary amount of time before you start asking for girls. And frankly, bottom line, and I've tested this out over many different industries. I've done this the years. It's the best time to ask for referral is right after you close the person. That very instinct. Why is that? Well, there's a couple of reasons. Number one is that when someone says yes, right? If once they say yes, in order to remain consistent with them saying yes, they won't think they made a smart decision. They can't say, oh yeah, I'll do it. I'll give you my money, but I'm not comfortable enough to recommend a friend. Rarely do you decide a person. Rather, that person is going to actually want to recommend this friend because he wants to form his own consensus. And those consensus is one of those core influencing traits. We want to be part of the crowd. We want to be part of the consensus, right? So it almost is an affirmation that he did the right thing. He made a good decision by going out there and getting someone else to do it too. That's why people will give you referrals right at the closing table. In addition, it's that moment after you close when you just load their actual threshold layer, then very high on the certain scale, that's when they're most apt to give you an referral. Don't fall into the trap. It's a weakness. It's just being weak. The second you close a deal, then first words out of your mouth, they're the great. You know, love is all by the way, right? Who else do you know that can help in the same way that it helped you? Now, who else can you refer to? Who else can, you know, I can help in the same way that I've just helped you? Remember, you just helped this person fill in. If you've done this right, you've built massive rapport along the way, right? You're in rapport, you're closed in rapport, because you can't close without rapport, right? This is the perfect time. Don't be shy. Don't like chicken answers. It's a mental thing. Just put your fucking back up with total comps. Say, oh, by the way, who else do you know that I can benefit in the same way that I helped you? And just shut up. Now, that being said, going forward, there are different levels of referrals. Like the best thing is, would be, listen, here's what I want you to do. I want you to take out your roll decks, or take out your smartphone, and I want you to actually dial number yourself right now in front of me. Here's what I want you to do, John. Take out your phone. I want you to hear your top three people. Oh, wait, right here. I want to wait here. I want you to dial them and tell them this is the greatest thing in the world, and then stand the phone to be empty, told them that I'm in front of you. That's your best referral, right? And then say, oh, yeah, I'll call my friend Jim, who will love this, and I'll tell Jim to give you calls. I don't care. Oh, yeah, sure. Here's my cell phone. Give it to Jim. Don't hold your fucking breath from Jim to call you. That's your work, right? And then in the middle of those is the place. Yeah, yeah, call this guy John Doe, and he has his phone, and he gives you the number. You might think I was kidding around when I had that first scenario, like, all right, give it. I want you to do John. Take out your freaking smartphone. Let me tell you something. If you do this right and you're in tight rapport with someone, you can get someone to take out their phone right now and stop calling people. I did this all the time. I'll be, I'm saying, I've been in someone's house. I closed them on their mortgage. That's what I was doing. The mortgage business is a perfect, bigger, referral business, right? Close them on their mortgage. I had not even refinanced their home yet. They had no idea if I was good, bad, or indifferent at what I did, but they were so confident based on how I sounded. We were in such tight rapport because of my focus of the second 10 and future pace of emotionally that a person who was typically hostile, when I woke, they were typically hostile somewhat, right? They were cooking me dinner. I'd be sitting there with a fucking meal out, wine here, and they're busy on their phone, holding their friends one after the next, saying what a great guy I am. I'm not a magician. I'm not, I'm not, it's not like I'm Harry Potter, so I guess the spell on them. It's very simple. I asked them to do it in a nice way. Now, take out your fucking smartphone and start dialing Bill. I'm like Bill, honestly, I can't tell you. My business, mostly it's on referral. You know, I call a call then probably, right? I still make, most of the business are full. I really appreciate if you, anyone who I could help them to say, oh yeah, I have my friend, you're great. Do me a favor, please. Can you please do me a favor? Look it right in the eye. Can you please give her a call? It's a big diff, don't flat out. You know it's a big difference if they hear it directly from you versus me calling them. Do you mind? What's so bad about that? Ask and you shall receive. Don't ask and you shall not receive. Just fucking ask them. Nicely, respectfully and with integrity. I'm not saying anything like, you know, you try to be on the road. I'm saying you have a great product. You help people. Why not help more people unless you don't believe in what you're telling? Then you should be selling it anyway. Ask for a referral on the spot and get them to dial that phone and pass you the phone or ask and die trying. Cause the worst that will happen, you know what I just, he's not right here. Here's his number. That's his default. They're not going to be mad at you if you do it the right way.
